flags.stage_neg1_fallback: stage_neg1 requested but pivot budget exhausted (NEG1_PIVOT_BUDGET=3): case 6b — Stage 0.5 reject is conjecture-level (tier_genuinely_below flagship floor), not a derivation slip; re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget since structure and correctness already pass.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp closed-form minimax-regret optimal policy and matching regret bound for offline policy learning under unmeasured confounding bounded by a Cinelli-Hazlett (2020) R-squared-sensitivity parameter: explicit Tikhonov-regularized inverse-propensity policy formula whose regret-bound dependence on the sensitivity parameter recovers Kallus-Zhou (2021) marginal-sensitivity-model bounds as the parameter goes to infinity and Adjaho-Christensen (2023) covariate-shift policy-evaluation bounds as the parameter goes to zero, with a sharpness witness via two boundary R-squared environments saturating the regret bound; extends Cinelli-Hazlett (2020, Section 7) sensitivity analysis from point-estimated effects to sharp policy-regret theory, the directly cited open direction in their conclusion
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue prior is pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1, which proposed a sharp joint-misspecification interval and non-rectangularity witness for offline policy value. This revision keeps that as background only: the flagship statement is a finite-policy calibration frontier among CH partial-R^2, MSM, and covariate-shift support-function regret endpoints and choice sets, not a joint propensity/outcome misspecification interval.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are CinelliHazlett2020 for partial-R^2 sensitivity, KallusZhou2021 and JinXuZhengZhou2025 for MSM robust policy learning, AdjahoChristensen2025 for external-validity policy choice, DiegertMastenPoirier2025 for sensitivity-parameter comparison, and HuangPimentel2025 for R^2-parameterized weighting sensitivity. The ε-gap is the observable τ_{Π_*} all-pairs threshold: none states a necessary-and-sufficient finite-policy condition under which local robust-regret endpoints and policy choice sets agree across these named sensitivity languages."}
seed_list: Prior work CinelliHazlett2020 and KallusZhou2021 would imply separate sensitivity languages for regression bias and policy regret. Our seed conjectures a sharp CH-calibrated minimax-regret policy formula, because residual partial-R-squared geometry induces an ellipsoidal inverse-propensity support function.

Prior work ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 and AdjahoChristensen2025 would imply that odds-ratio hidden-confounding and covariate-shift ambiguity are distinct policy-risk languages. Our seed conjectures a bridge theorem with CH zero- and infinite-strength limits, because both become one-dimensional support functions on local policy scores after compatibility normalization.

Prior proposal pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 would predict a finite-grid joint interval but not a CH-regret formula. Our seed conjectures that the same compatibility obstruction becomes an R-squared sharpness witness, because two boundary latent environments must paste through the same factual law.

Prior proposal pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1 would imply pointwise sharp CQTE bounds can be selected independently. Our seed conjectures process non-pasting under marginal sensitivity, because one bounded-weight latent distribution must support all quantile choices simultaneously.

Prior proposal pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope_v1 would imply pairwise IV sensitivity frontiers are enough. Our seed conjectures a nonrectangular ATE sign frontier with defier, never-taker, and c-dependence constraints, because the three caps bind on mixed principal-stratum faces.

Prior work KitagawaTetenov2018 and AtheyWager2021 would predict welfare maximization from point-identified scores. Our seed conjectures minimax-regret treatment choice over set-identified welfare intervals, because lower-welfare and upper-regret criteria select different rules under hidden confounding.

Prior work DornGuoKallus2025 and JinXuZhengZhou2025 would predict that sharp ATE sensitivity scores can feed robust policy learning. Our seed conjectures an orthogonal sharp regret score after inner worst-case optimization, because the adversarial weights and selected policy co-move with nuisance estimates.

Prior work Manski1990 and ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 would predict intersection-bound inference for fixed conditional restrictions. Our seed conjectures post-policy-search intersection bias, because optimizing over policy classes changes which covariate-stratum bounds bind.

Prior work CinelliHazlett2020 would predict robustness values for changing a coefficient sign. Our seed conjectures a policy-regret robustness value for loss of status-quo improvement, because the overturning event is a support-function crossing rather than a scalar coefficient crossing.

Prior work KallusZhou2021 would predict worst-case regret over propensity-weight uncertainty. Our seed conjectures a smooth-to-kinked phase transition under CH regularization, because the Tikhonov inverse score hits the policy ellipsoid boundary before the marginal-sensitivity face is reached.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CinelliHazlett2020 and KallusZhou2021 would imply separate sensitivity languages for regression bias and policy regret. Our seed conjectures a sharp CH-calibrated minimax-regret policy formula, because residual partial-R-squared geometry induces an ellipsoidal inverse-propensity support function.

anchor_paper.author: Cinelli and Hazlett / Kallus and Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Cinelli-Hazlett introduce partial-R-squared omitted-variable sensitivity; Kallus-Zhou minimize worst-case estimated regret over propensity-weight uncertainty sets.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is not a generic assumption weakening; the claimed phenomenon is a new CH support-function geometry yielding a Tikhonov policy rule.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from KallusZhou2021 because their uncertainty set already gives minimax-regret policy learning.
seed_details.rebuttal: Kallus-Zhou use propensity-weight/MSM uncertainty, while the seed asks for a CH residual partial-R-squared support function and sharp boundary environments.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 and AdjahoChristensen2025 would imply that odds-ratio hidden-confounding and covariate-shift ambiguity are distinct policy-risk languages. Our seed conjectures a bridge theorem with CH zero- and infinite-strength limits, because both become one-dimensional support functions on local policy scores after compatibility normalization.

anchor_paper.author: Zhao, Small, and Bhattacharya / Adjaho and Christensen
anchor_paper.claim: MSM relaxes no unmeasured confounding through odds-ratio bounds; externally valid policy choice studies shifts across target populations.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it is a limiting-equivalence and incompatibility claim among named ambiguity languages, not applying one method to another setting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just parameter rescaling between sensitivity models.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap payload explicitly notes compatibility is needed; the proposed bridge requires a shared latent law and support-function limit, not scalar rescaling.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 would predict a finite-grid joint interval but not a CH-regret formula. Our seed conjectures that the same compatibility obstruction becomes an R-squared sharpness witness, because two boundary latent environments must paste through the same factual law.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: sharp joint-misspecification variational interval (NOT YET PROVED); reviewer requested finite-grid compatibility lemma.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it uses prior-proposal compatibility as a warning while changing the kernel to CH policy-regret sharpness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is the same joint-misspecification interval under a new parameter name.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposal replaces finite-grid propensity/outcome envelopes with CH partial-R-squared residual geometry and a Tikhonov minimax policy formula.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1 would imply pointwise sharp CQTE bounds can be selected independently. Our seed conjectures process non-pasting under marginal sensitivity, because one bounded-weight latent distribution must support all quantile choices simultaneously.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: finite-support CQTE process compatibility (NOT YET PROVED).

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the surprise is process compatibility failure, not a CATE/QTE restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just finite-dimensional LP feasibility.
seed_details.rebuttal: The open gap is an if-and-only-if union-of-polytopes compatibility criterion separating process sharpness from Cartesian pointwise hulls.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope_v1 would imply pairwise IV sensitivity frontiers are enough. Our seed conjectures a nonrectangular ATE sign frontier with defier, never-taker, and c-dependence constraints, because the three caps bind on mixed principal-stratum faces.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: non-rectangular ATE sign frontier (NOT YET PROVED).

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it alleges a named nonrectangular frontier with mixed binding constraints, not a generic IV LP extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: Balke-Pearl LP sharpness makes this automatic.
seed_details.rebuttal: LP existence is known; the seed asks for an open-cell strict separation from pairwise sensitivity frontiers.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work KitagawaTetenov2018 and AtheyWager2021 would predict welfare maximization from point-identified scores. Our seed conjectures minimax-regret treatment choice over set-identified welfare intervals, because lower-welfare and upper-regret criteria select different rules under hidden confounding.

anchor_paper.author: Kitagawa and Tetenov / Athey and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: EWM and doubly robust policy learning optimize point-identified welfare scores under identifying assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the phenomenon is criterion separation under set identification, not deriving ATT/CATE from ATE.
seed_details.referee_objection: Manski and Stoye already study minimax-regret treatment choice under partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: True for treatment choice generally; the remaining gap is sharp hidden-confounding policy-value intervals for modern policy classes, so this seed is lower ranked.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DornGuoKallus2025 and JinXuZhengZhou2025 would predict that sharp ATE sensitivity scores can feed robust policy learning. Our seed conjectures an orthogonal sharp regret score after inner worst-case optimization, because the adversarial weights and selected policy co-move with nuisance estimates.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn, Guo, and Kallus / Jin, Xu, Zheng, and Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Dorn-Guo-Kallus derive doubly-valid/doubly-sharp sensitivity bounds; Jin-Xu-Zheng-Zhou derive robust welfare criteria and regret bounds under MSM.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the claimed obstruction is coupled inner-outer optimization and orthogonality after maximization, not a routine estimator extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an estimation theorem downstream of already-known sharp bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The robust policy and adversarial weights are jointly selected, so ATE orthogonality does not immediately imply uniform policy-regret orthogonality.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Manski1990 and ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 would predict intersection-bound inference for fixed conditional restrictions. Our seed conjectures post-policy-search intersection bias, because optimizing over policy classes changes which covariate-stratum bounds bind.

anchor_paper.author: Manski / Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen
anchor_paper.claim: Manski gives nonparametric treatment-effect bounds; Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen develop inference for intersection bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked in principle, but lower ranked: inference after search risks being an empirical-process extension unless a sharp binding-set phase transition is isolated.
seed_details.referee_objection: Post-selection uniform inference is a known empirical-process problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed novelty would need a partial-ID binding-set phenomenon; not chosen as flagship here.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CinelliHazlett2020 would predict robustness values for changing a coefficient sign. Our seed conjectures a policy-regret robustness value for loss of status-quo improvement, because the overturning event is a support-function crossing rather than a scalar coefficient crossing.

anchor_paper.author: Cinelli and Hazlett
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces robustness values and partial-R-squared sensitivity parameters for omitted-variable bias in regression.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it is not just a new target sign; the event is a policy-regret support crossing requiring sharp policy-value bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is merely applying CH robustness values to a new estimand.
seed_details.rebuttal: The policy-regret crossing involves supremum over policies and latent environments, so the scalar coefficient robustness-value algebra does not directly apply.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work KallusZhou2021 would predict worst-case regret over propensity-weight uncertainty. Our seed conjectures a smooth-to-kinked phase transition under CH regularization, because the Tikhonov inverse score hits the policy ellipsoid boundary before the marginal-sensitivity face is reached.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus and Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Minimizes worst-case estimated regret over propensity-weight uncertainty sets and proves minimax policy-learning guarantees.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it alleges a regime-change boundary in a named published workflow rather than iid-to-general or assumption weakening.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is convex optimization geometry, not a causal identification result.
seed_details.rebuttal: The kink location is meaningful only if the CH support function is sharp over compatible latent environments; that compatibility component is the causal kernel.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Cinelli-Hazlett (2020, JRSS-B) give the partial-R-squared sensitivity language for omitted-variable bias, but the object is a regression effect rather than a policy-regret functional.
2. Kallus-Zhou (2021, Management Science) make hidden-confounding policy improvement a minimax-regret problem over propensity-weight uncertainty sets.
3. Zhao-Small-Bhattacharya (2019, JRSS-B), Yadlowsky et al. (2022, Annals of Statistics), and Dorn-Guo-Kallus (2025, JASA) sharpen the marginal-sensitivity-model side of effect bounds.
4. Jin-Xu-Zheng-Zhou (2025, arXiv) pushes MSM policy learning toward efficient robust welfare and regret, but still in the odds-ratio sensitivity language.
5. Kitagawa-Tetenov (2018, Econometrica) and Athey-Wager (2021, Econometrica) give point-identified policy-learning baselines whose regret theory must be reworked under set identification.
6. Adjaho-Christensen (2025, arXiv) and Christensen-Connault (2023, Econometrica) supply adjacent covariate-shift and latent-distribution ambiguity tools.
7. Stoye-style partial-ID treatment choice warns that minimax-regret rules can be nonunique or randomized when welfare is weakly identified.
8. Manski (1990, AER) and Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen (2013, Econometrica) anchor the study-pipeline gaps on nonparametric bounds and intersection-bound inference.
9. The strongest policy-regret gap is the missing compatibility theorem linking CH R-squared budgets, MSM odds-ratio budgets, and covariate-shift limits.
10. Across sources, the recurring obstacle is sharp latent-environment pasting under multiple simultaneous sensitivity or ambiguity restrictions.

banked_reason: Both productive angles ACCEPT -0.5 flagship, both REJECT 0.5 with proposal_promise_gap=tier_genuinely_below: sensitivity-analysis algebra has subfield-tier substance even when derived faithfully.
